title: Book chapter outline (arabic-authoring-suite)
description: Schema for a long-form Arabic book chapter (classical/opinion register). Stricter than article: 4-8 sections, each may contain subsections, total budget 3000-8000 words. Bibliography minimum of 10 sources enforced by author.py before this outline is even drafted.

chapter_number.description: Chapter ordinal within the book. Used by v0.2+ for cross-chapter terminology continuity (a future feature).

epigraph_ar.description: Optional opening epigraph (often Quranic or classical poetry). If present, will be wrapped by sacred_text_guard during drafting and humanizer passes.

register.description: Classical register is the canonical choice; opinion register is acceptable for argumentative chapters.

thematic_throughlines.description: Cross-section threads the chapter develops. The humanizer-as-gate uses these to detect ring-composition violations: a throughline mentioned in section 1 but never returned to.

intent.description: Book chapters demand longer intent statements than articles (min 30 vs 20). The cognitive structure must survive the larger word budget.

source_refs.description: Book chapter sections require AT LEAST 2 sources each (vs 1 for article sections). Single-source sections become opinion masquerading as scholarship — the chapter-level minimum of 10 sources should distribute across sections.

subsections.description: Optional nested subsections (H3-level). Allowed for sections >= 1500 words.

source_refs.description: Subsections inherit parent section's source_refs if empty; the drafter validates at runtime.
